engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Spacia Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Suzuki Spacia in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for grey import kei vehicles.

cards.altText: Suzuki Spacia R06A engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
productionHistory: G, X, Custom, Turbo variants (MK2 chassis, 2017-present)
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise from 55k miles on high-rev kei engines.
VVT solenoid fault - causes rough idle and loss of power on import units.
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear on high-mileage Japanese imports.

cards.altText: Suzuki Spacia R06D engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
productionHistory: SHVS mild hybrid system equipped models
knownFailures: ISG motor calibration fault - mild hybrid system warning light on dashboard.
12V auxiliary battery degradation - affects SHVS start-stop function.
Timing chain tensioner wear - shared R06A family issue on higher mileage units.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,495-£2,295 (supply only) Estimated from comparable R06D units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Suzuki Spacia R06A-T engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo actuator sticking - boost pressure faults on high-rev kei turbo units.
Intercooler hose cracking - boost leaks common on compact engine bays.
Timing chain tensioner wear - high-rev operation accelerates tensioner fatigue.

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Spacia Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Spacia's R06A and R06D kei engines are generally reliable but known issues like timing chain tensioner wear and CVT shudder can drive replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why R06A Engines Develop Rattling Noise
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Spacia G, X, Custom, Turbo (2013-present, R06A & R06D)
problems.rootCause: High-rev kei engine operation accelerates tensioner wear; Japanese-spec parts may not match UK driving patterns.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner and guide rails; inspect chain wear
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, DVLA compliant
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage imports; includes 12-month warranty and all wear items addressed

problems.recommendation: On a Suzuki Spacia worth £3,000-£6,000 as an import, preventative tensioner replacement makes sense below 60k miles. Beyond that, a rebuilt R06A with upgraded components offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt R06A engine prices for your Suzuki Spacia

problems.h4: CVT Transmission Shudder - Auto Variant Jerking Under Load
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Spacia CVT variants (2013-present, R06A & R06D with auto)
problems.rootCause: CVT fluid degradation and belt wear in compact kei transmission; exacerbated by stop-start urban driving.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain/refill with spec fluid; reset adaptation values; road test
repairOptions.longevity: May resolve mild shudder; not effective if belt damage has occurred

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine & CVT Package
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt R06A with CVT compatibility verification; calibrated for UK use
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for severe shudder; ensures drivetrain harmony and import compliance

problems.recommendation: CVT shudder on a Spacia import often indicates advanced wear. Given the compact kei design complexity, a rebuilt engine with CVT calibration verification typically offers better reliability than partial repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Suzuki Spacia CVT engine replacement

problems.h4: Oil Consumption Increase - Blue Smoke on High-Mileage Imports
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Spacia all variants (2013-present, R06A high-mileage units)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear on high-rev kei engines; Japanese auction grades may not reflect UK mileage accumulation patterns.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, valve cover seals; monitor consumption
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation only; does not address root piston ring wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt R06A with Reinforced Rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded piston rings per Suzuki technical bulletins
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution; eliminates consumption issue with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: Oil consumption on a high-mileage Spacia import signals internal wear. A rebuilt R06A with reinforced rings addresses the root cause and provides warranty coverage that patch repairs cannot match.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt R06A engine prices for your Suzuki Spacia

problems.h4: VVT Solenoid Fault - Rough Idle and Power Loss
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Spacia G, X, Custom, Hybrid (2013-present, R06A & R06D with VVT)
problems.rootCause: Solenoid clogging from oil deposits; compact kei engine bay heat accelerates electrical component degradation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VVT solenoid; perform Japanese-spec ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for isolated solenoid failure; verify oil quality to prevent recurrence

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with VVT System Check
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with full VVT system verification and calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if multiple VVT-related codes present or engine has other wear indicators

problems.recommendation: VVT solenoid faults on Spacia imports are often isolated and cost-effective to repair. However, if accompanied by timing noise or oil consumption, a rebuilt engine provides comprehensive protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for R06A VVT solenoid repair or replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Spacia
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain rattles, CVT shudder, oil consumption, and VVT faults are the most common triggers for Suzuki Spacia engine replacement enquiries in the UK. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand grey import kei engines. Every rebuilt unit includes upgraded components addressing original failure points, DVLA compliance documentation support, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get your Spacia back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Spacia Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Spacia and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Suzuki Spacia 0.66L Petrol Engines
items.description: All Suzuki Spacia variants sold in the UK use the compact 0.66L (658cc) kei engine family - either naturally aspirated R06A, turbocharged R06A-T, or mild hybrid R06D SHVS. This displacement is mandated by Japanese kei car regulations and delivers efficient performance for urban driving, though high-rev operation requires diligent maintenance on imported units.

CVT automatic transmission standard on most import configurations

knownFor: Compact kei tall wagon design ideal for UK urban driving
Timing chain tensioner wear emerges as common high-mileage issue
Japanese auction grade verification critical for import condition assessment

R06D SHVS mild hybrid system introduced on Hybrid and Custom Hybrid variants
Improved emissions calibration for better UK MoT compliance on later imports
knownFor: SHVS mild hybrid improves urban fuel economy without plug-in complexity
R06D shares R06A timing chain tensioner vulnerability on high-mileage units
ISG motor calibration faults emerge as hybrid-specific replacement trigger

DVLA compliance documentation support becomes critical for late-model imports

Core return incentives help offset replacement cost on scarce late-model units

faq.h2: Suzuki Spacia Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Suzuki Spacia engine replacement cost?
items.answer: Suzuki Spacia engine replacement costs typically range from £795 for used units to £1,995 for rebuilt R06A/R06D engines, supply only. Prices vary by variant, mileage, and failure severity.
keyPoints: Used R06A engines start around £795 with auction verification
Rebuilt R06A/R06D units average £1,295-£1,995 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£850 depending on location
DVLA compliance documentation included in all quotes
items.cta: Get your free Suzuki Spacia engine quote in 60 seconds

items.question: What engine code is in my Suzuki Spacia?
items.answer: Most UK Suzuki Spacia imports use the R06A 0.66L kei engine; later models (2017+) may have R06D mild hybrid. Check your Japanese export certificate or V5C for confirmation.
keyPoints: R06A powers G, X, Custom variants (2013-present)
R06D SHVS fitted to Hybrid/Custom Hybrid (2017-present)
Export certificate lists engine code in section 2
Enter registration above for instant DVLA code verification
items.cta: Verify your Spacia engine code with our free lookup tool

items.question: Will a reconditioned Suzuki Spacia engine fit my grey import?
items.answer: Yes - all rebuilt R06A/R06D engines supplied through EngineMarket are calibrated for direct fitment to UK-registered Suzuki Spacia imports with DVLA compliance documentation.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units match original Japanese-spec mounting points
ECU calibration verified for UK emissions and MoT compliance

items.question: How do I know if my Suzuki Spacia needs a new engine?
items.answer: Key signs include persistent rattling (timing chain), blue exhaust smoke (oil consumption), CVT shudder under load, or warning lights that persist after basic diagnostics.
keyPoints: Rattling from front of engine suggests timing chain tensioner wear
Blue smoke on acceleration indicates piston ring wear
CVT shudder may signal transmission or engine calibration issues
Free diagnostic code check available with quote request
